---
title: "Financial Sentiment Analysis: Predicting Market Reactions"
author: "Manasvi Vardham"
date: today
categories: [machine learning, NLP, finance]
description: >
  Comparing FinBERT and VADER to evaluate robustness in financial sentiment classification.
---

## 📌 Project Overview

This project explores how financial sentiment impacts stock behavior by comparing two popular NLP approaches:

- **FinBERT** (domain-specific transformer model)
- **VADER** (rule-based sentiment model)

The goal was to evaluate model robustness and predictive signal strength when applied to financial text datasets.

---

## 🔍 Methodology

1. Cleaned and preprocessed financial text data
2. Generated sentiment scores using:
   - FinBERT
   - VADER
3. Compared predicted sentiment vs. actual market movement
4. Evaluated signal strength using correlation and regression analysis

---

## 📊 Key Findings

- FinBERT showed stronger contextual understanding.
- VADER performed well on clearly polarized statements but struggled with financial nuance.
- Sentiment signal had moderate predictive power depending on volatility regime.

---

## 🛠 Tools Used

- Python
- HuggingFace Transformers
- Pandas
- Scikit-learn
- Matplotlib

---

## 📈 Next Steps

- Test multi-day lag effects
- Expand dataset to additional tickers
- Explore ensemble sentiment modeling
